amendment_if_yes_amended: Publish v0.1.0 but exclude swarm agent logic and memory modules from npm/GitHub Packages; ship only CLI shell + mock connectors. Real swarm integration remains private until identity.md access control is enforced.
rationale: The CLI is technically stable (88 passing tests) and v0.1.0 signals momentum. However, publishing swarm perspectives — even as code — risks exposing Constitution-governed behavior patterns (e.g., shame-free language enforcement, animation safety) before runtime access controls exist. Swarm logic must not be publicly inspectable or reusable outside VOLAURA’s user-consented boundary. Shipping mock connectors allows ecosystem testing without architectural exposure.
risk_if_other_side_wins: Delay erodes trust in delivery velocity; prolonged secrecy increases pressure to over-polish. But rushed full release risks swarm logic being forked into red/controlling/shaming interfaces — direct violation of Laws 1,3,5.
